rationale: The cited paper appears only once in the citing article, in a grouped citation within the common method bias safeguards section (with Podsakoff et al. 2003). It is used as general support for standard survey design recommendations such as separating measures and ensuring confidentiality, rather than as a specific framework that structures the model or hypotheses. The citing paper does not build its core theory, empirical strategy, or contribution on the cross-sectional versus longitudinal guidelines from the cited work.
